item.title: Trump’s ‘Regime Change’ Swerve
item.description: Trump’s statement about Iran is premature.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: HHS Is Exiling Top Officials to the Indian Health Service
item.description: The Indian Health Service needs more clinical workers. The federal health officials being transferred to the agency don’t fit that bill.
item.dc:creator: Katherine J. Wu

item.title: A Boring Video Game I Can’t Put Down
item.description: Playing the role of a rental-store owner reveals the thrill in mundanity.
item.dc:creator: David Sims

item.title: The Real Religious ‘Renewal’ Happening in Gen Z
item.description: Some pastors and politicians claim that a Christian revival is afoot among young Americans. Nationwide data tell a different story.
item.dc:creator: Luis Parrales

item.title: No Good Way Out
item.description: Trump has options to end the war with Iran, but they all come with serious liabilities.
item.dc:creator: Nancy A. Youssef

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Board Games
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: If You Need a Laptop, Buy It Now
item.description: Electronics are getting more expensive and worse. Blame the AI boom.
item.dc:creator: Hana Kiros

item.title: The Family That Decided to Have Their Stomachs Removed
item.description: Māori families with a mutation for aggressive gastric cancer have had their stomachs preemptively removed. How do you live without one?
item.dc:creator: Sarah Zhang

item.title: Trump Is Flailing on Iran
item.description: He can’t scare Iran and reassure the markets at the same time. But he’s trying.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: What Maxxing Reveals About Life Online
item.description: “Looksmaxxing” and its cousins are fake trends but real expressions of the internet’s intrinsic drive toward extremism.
item.dc:creator: Ian Bogost

item.title: The Hardest Job in Europe
item.description: Ukraine’s ambassador to Hungary represents a government that has become Viktor Orbán’s primary target.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Stanley-Becker

item.title: The Making of a Diagnostic Mind
item.description: “To me, the concept of the master diagnostician is that you’re never good enough,” one doctor said.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Sifferlin

item.title: America Needs to Get Serious About Drones
item.description: The new age of war is already here, swarming over Barksdale Air Force Base.
item.dc:creator: Brynn Tannehill

item.title: MAHA Has Been Given an Impossible Task
item.description: The Trump administration seems to be leaning on the movement as a distraction.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bartlett

item.title: Pete Hegseth Is Vice Signaling
item.description: The defense secretary is trolling America.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nichols

item.title: A Game Plan for the AI Boom
item.description: Ten years ago, AlphaGo trounced human competitors—and its legacy is still present in today’s most advanced bots.
item.dc:creator: Matteo Wong

item.title: Does the Constitution Protect This Congresswoman From Trump?
item.description: LaMonica McIver of New Jersey is arguing that the Department of Justice can’t prosecute her for an incident last May at an ICE facility.
item.dc:creator: Quinta Jurecic

item.title: AtlanticTrivia for March 30, 2026: Sondheim Tunes
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Will People Ever Stop Eating Animals?
item.description: Bruce Friedrich has devoted his life to reducing American meat consumption—and he isn’t giving up just yet.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Zaleski

item.title: How Long Can You Live Your Ideals?
item.description: Stories about revolutionaries seem to entrance readers and moviegoers alike—especially if they don’t end well.
item.dc:creator: Lily Meyer
